heading: Start Inside
body: **Inner parentheses** mark the deepest grouped part of a formula. In a truth table, that part is evaluated first and placed in its own operational column.

examples.text: In $\sim (P \wedge Q) \vee R$, the inner parenthesis is $(P \wedge Q)$.
In $\sim (P \wedge Q) \vee R$, the first column to compute is the whole formula.

note: This follows the same grouping rules used in symbolic translation.

heading: One Column Per Part
body: An **operational column** records the truth values of one compound part. Every inner grouped expression gets its own column before larger expressions are evaluated.

examples.text: For $\sim (P \wedge Q) \vee R$, make a column for $(P \wedge Q)$ before making a column for $\sim (P \wedge Q)$.
For $\sim (P \wedge Q) \vee R$, skip $(P \wedge Q)$ and jump straight to $\vee$.

heading: Evaluate Deepest First
body: To evaluate a **nested expression**, find the deepest parenthesized part and compute it row by row. Use only the columns that already exist for its immediate components.

examples.text: In $((P \vee Q) \rightarrow R)$, compute $(P \vee Q)$ before the conditional.
In $((P \vee Q) \rightarrow R)$, compute the conditional before $(P \vee Q)$.

heading: Conjunction Example
body: For $(P \wedge Q)$, the column is true only when both $P$ and $Q$ are true in the same row.

examples.text: If $P$ is `T T F F` and $Q$ is `T F T F`, then $(P \wedge Q)$ is `T F F F`.
If $P$ is `T T F F` and $Q$ is `T F T F`, then $(P \wedge Q)$ is `T T T F`.

heading: Do Not Skip Levels
body: A common mistake is mixing several operations into one step. If a formula has an inner grouped part, compute that part first, even if the next operator looks easy.

examples.text: In $\sim (P \wedge Q) \vee R$, compute $(P \wedge Q)$, then $\sim (P \wedge Q)$, then the final disjunction.
In $\sim (P \wedge Q) \vee R$, combine $Q$ and $R$ first because they are close together.

note: Truth-table order follows structure, not visual convenience.

heading: Core Method
body: Build truth tables from the inside out. Compute the deepest parenthesized expression first, place it in its own column, then move outward step by step.
